canonical_target: Beelink Mini PC — 4-core Debian LXC (isolated benchmark instance)
driver_notes: Run wrk on the same host as Tardigrade against loopback. The benchmark instance runs on a dedicated port that never touches the production Tardigrade process (port 8443). Idle the machine before each run and use --idle-check to gate on load average.
policy: Canonical performance numbers for Tardigrade. All published req/s and latency figures are captured here. Use --runs 3 minimum for release baselines to catch measurement noise. Compare each release against the previous saved baseline JSON.

hardware.scheduler: CFS (Linux real scheduler; macOS QoS does not apply)

notes: Results from a macOS laptop are not comparable — macOS cores are faster and the QoS scheduler produces different tail latencies.
A periodic top/ps sampler co-scheduled with wrk collapsed throughput ~5× in early experiments; use --pid-file or --pid to let the harness sample instead.
Use --runs 3 for release baselines and inspect rps_stddev; a stddev > 10% of rps_mean suggests load contamination.
